item.title: _status_output calls count_by_status 6 times — consolidate to one jq pass per file
item.intent: _status_output extracts all item counts from each backlog file in a single jq call per file; no redundant file parses.
item.description: _status_output (called by cmd_status and cmd_status --watch loop) calls count_by_status three times per backlog file — once each for 'todo', 'in-progress', and 'done' — yielding 6 jq subprocess forks total. All three counts can be extracted in a single jq call per file using group_by or select, then stored in local variables. This saves 4 subprocess forks per status refresh and is especially noticeable in --watch mode.
steps: Replace the three count_by_status calls for backlog_file with a single jq invocation that extracts todo/in-progress/done counts in one pass
Replace the three count_by_status calls for bugs_file with a single jq invocation in the same manner
Store counts in local variables and format the output string from those variables
Verify: aishore status shows correct counts for each status
Verify: shellcheck passes with no new findings
acceptanceCriteria: backlog_file counts are extracted in a single jq call (not three)
bugs_file counts are extracted in a single jq call (not three)
aishore status output format is unchanged
